name: Route of the Villages
name_original: Rota das Aldeias — percurso circular PR3 CSC no Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais
description: Hike the Rota das Aldeias PR3 CSC in Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, a moderate 8.5-mile loop with coastal views and varied terrain.
region_intro: Hiking around Alcabideche offers access to diverse landscapes within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, a region characterized by coastal cliffs, sand dunes, and dense woodlands. The area provides a blend of environments, from challenging trails along the Atlantic coastline to paths winding through lush forests. Its geographical position allows for exploration of varied ecosystems, including geological formations dating back to the Jurassic and Cretaceous periods. Best hiking trails around Alcabideche The most popular hiking route is Rio da Mula e Memorial dos Soldados — circular route PR 6 SNT in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route explores varied terrain within the natural park, featuring both forested sections and open areas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Rota do Litoral do Guincho — circular route PR4 CSC in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, a moderate 6.0 miles (9.6 km) path. This trail offers coastal views along the Guincho area, showcasing the region's sea cliffs and beaches. Local hikers also love the Rota das Aldeias — circular route PR3 CSC in Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais, a 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Alcabideche is defined by coastal paths, dense woodlands, and varied terrain within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more strenuous climbs. The routes in Alcabideche are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcabideche's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alcabideche?
A: Alcabideche offers nearly 100 hiking routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with 39 easy trails, 42 moderate options, and 18 more challenging routes to explore.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes near Alcabideche?
A: The terrain around Alcabideche is highly diverse, largely due to its location within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. Hikers can experience everything from rugged coastal cliffs and expansive sand dunes to dense forests and tranquil woodlands. Many trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379859931/rio-da-mula-e-memorial-dos-soldados-circular-route-pr-6-snt-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio da Mula e Memorial dos Soldados circular route&lt;/a&gt;, feature varied landscapes, including both forested sections and open areas.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Alcabideche area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Alcabideche are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379748461/rota-do-litoral-do-guincho-circular-route-pr4-csc-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rota do Litoral do Guincho&lt;/a&gt;, which provides coastal views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379746459/rota-das-aldeias-circular-route-pr3-csc-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rota das Aldeias&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through the natural park.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to see while hiking?
A: Hiking around Alcabideche offers access to numerous points of interest. Within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, you can find dramatic sea cliffs, especially along coastal routes near Praia do Guincho. Inland, trails often lead through lush woodlands and past geological formations dating back to the Jurassic and Cretaceous periods. You might also encounter historical sites like the Peninha Sanctuary, offering panoramic views. For specific attractions, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mula River Dam&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8001951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall Lake&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options near Alcabideche?
A: Yes, Alcabideche and the surrounding Sintra-Cascais Natural Park offer several easy and accessible paths suitable for families. While specific family-designated routes aren't listed, the 39 easy trails available provide gentler terrain and shorter distances, perfect for walks with children. The region's local greenways also offer convenient options for casual strolls.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcabideche?
A: The region's mild climate makes it suitable for hiking year-round. However, spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are particularly pleasant, with comfortable temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, especially inland, but coastal routes benefit from sea breezes. Winter offers cooler temperatures, ideal for longer hikes, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Alcabideche?
A: The trails around Alcabideche are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 460 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the dramatic coastal views to the serene forest paths within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. The variety of terrain and well-maintained routes are also often highlighted.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the ocean?
A: Absolutely. Alcabideche's proximity to the Atlantic coast means many trails offer stunning ocean vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379748461/rota-do-litoral-do-guincho-circular-route-pr4-csc-in-parque-natural-sintra-cascais" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rota do Litoral do Guincho&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for coastal views, showcasing the region's impressive sea cliffs and beaches like Praia do Guincho. The Peninha Circular Trail also provides rewarding views that can extend to the distant ocean on clear days.

Q: Are there any lakes or waterfalls accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, which borders Alcabideche, features several water bodies. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345009" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mula River Dam&lt;/a&gt;, which is a notable highlight in the area. There's also a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8001951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall Lake&lt;/a&gt; that can be explored, offering tranquil spots amidst the natural scenery.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles I can visit near the hiking areas?
A: Alcabideche's location provides easy access to the rich historical and cultural attractions of nearby Sintra. While hiking, you can extend your trip to visit magnificent sites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/202786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pena Palace&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/205133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Moors&lt;/a&gt;, and the enchanting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/185113" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quinta da Regaleira&lt;/a&gt;. These are all within a short distance and offer unique experiences beyond the trails.

Q: What is the difficulty level of hikes in Alcabideche?
A: The hiking trails around Alcabideche cater to a wide range of abilities. You'll find a balanced distribution of difficulty, with 39 easy routes perfect for casual walkers, 42 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge, and 18 difficult options for experienced hikers looking for strenuous climbs and longer distances.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to viewpoints with panoramic scenery?
A: Yes, the region is known for its scenic viewpoints. The Peninha Circular Trail, for instance, offers rewarding views from the historic Peninha Sanctuary, where on a clear day, you can see landmarks like Pena Palace, the Moorish Castle, and Cabo da Roca. Short viewpoint walks around Alcabideche also provide quick opportunities for panoramic photos and scenic vistas of the surrounding natural park and coastline.
tips: Malveira da Serra is a town located entirely within the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. Starting point for several trails in the region, as well as being a charming and peaceful village.

The Peninha Sanctuary, located in the Sintra Mountains in the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park, in Portugal, is located at an altitude of 448 meters, offering views over the coast and inland. In addition to a baroque chapel completed in 1710, the site is home to the Peninha Palace, dating from 1918, and remains of a hermitage. Currently, the interiors of the chapel and palace are not open to visitors.
Point on the Peninha trail - close to the sanctuary - where you can see a good part of the coast. As you walk, the landscape changes, at certain times you can see from Cabo da Roca to Cascais.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike this route and what is its difficulty?
faqs.answer: The Rota das Aldeias is classified as a moderate hike. It typically takes around 4 to 5 hours to complete the 13.7 km (8.5 miles) loop, with an elevation gain of approximately 435 meters.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Rota das Aldeias?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain, including rural and mountain paths. You'll encounter a mix of unpaved and paved sections, natural ground, gravel, and some asphalt. It winds through planted forests, native vegetation groves, Atlantic-Mediterranean scrubland, and agricultural landscapes with small farmsteads.

faqs.question: Where does the Rota das Aldeias trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The circular route typically starts at the Chapel of Malveira da Serra. Parking is usually available in or near the village, though specific parking areas may vary. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best seasons to hike the Rota das Aldeias are generally spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring rain, making some sections muddy. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Rota das Aldeias trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Rota das Aldeias is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through a Natural Park and agricultural areas, it's recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect wildlife and respect local farm animals. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse sights, including the charming village of Malveira da Serra, the historic Peninha Sanctuary, and breathtaking views of Cascais and Cabo da Roca. You'll also encounter ancient lime kilns, quarries, old windmills, and picturesque streams, showcasing the region's natural beauty and cultural heritage.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There is no specific permit or entrance fee required to hike the Rota das Aldeias or to access the general areas of the Parque Natural Sintra-Cascais. However, always respect park regulations, such as staying on marked trails and not disturbing wildlife or vegetation.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: While hiking, you might spot various birds of prey, red foxes, and 'sardão' lizards. The diverse habitats, from forests to scrubland, support a range of local fauna.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Rota das Aldeias (PR3 CSC) covers 97% of its own designated route, but it also shares sections with other trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Rota do Ocidente - Etapa 3, Trilho das Areias, and Rota do Ocidente - Etapa 2, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through rural villages like Malveira da Serra, where you may find local cafes or restaurants. It's a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially if you plan to rely on them for refreshments or a meal after your hike.

faqs.question: What makes the Rota das Aldeias particularly notable?
faqs.answer: This trail is notable for its journey through the 'saloia' countryside, preserving ancestral customs and rural traditions. It offers a unique blend of stunning coastal and mountain scenery, historical insights into agricultural practices, and landmarks like the Peninha Sanctuary, making it a culturally and visually enriching experience.

waypoints.name: View of Cascais and Cabo da Roca
